assertions.expectation: Distinguishes between dev (Rapid) and prod (Stable) clusters in the checklists
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions upgrading dev clusters first on Rapid channel before prod, and suggests staggering prod cluster upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recognizes Autopilot limitations (no node management, no SSH, mandatory resource requests)
assertions.reason: Includes Autopilot-specific section noting node pools are managed by Google and mentions focus on workload readiness rather than node management

assertions.expectation: Includes API deprecation checks with kubectl commands
assertions.reason: Mentions API removals verification and includes kubectl commands for version checking and validation

assertions.expectation: Mentions mandatory resource requests as an Autopilot-specific concern
assertions.reason: While it mentions Autopilot resource limits and optimization, it does not specifically address mandatory resource requests requirement

assertions.expectation: Pre-upgrade checklist includes workload readiness items (PDBs, bare pods, graceful shutdown)
assertions.reason: Includes PodDisruptionBudgets verification, multi-replica deployments check, and workload assessment items

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes version verification commands
assertions.reason: Explicitly includes 'kubectl version' command to validate cluster version

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes workload health validation
assertions.reason: Comprehensive workload health checks including deployments, pods, services, and application testing sections

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing on dev clusters before prod auto-upgrade
assertions.reason: Explicitly states to upgrade dev clusters first and run full testing suite before prod upgrades
Includes monitoring dashboard preparation, performance metrics comparison, and logging system validation
